_comment: Pin-of-record for the SOVEREIGN WEAVE (CELE-t373): the OpenCode + Ollama + Qwen blend Celeborn orchestrates but never owns. Prose contract: references/weave-contract.md. UNLIKE the cmm/infisical pins, the weave installs from each project's OWN official channel (OpenCode installer, Ollama installer, `ollama pull`) — Celeborn NEVER vendors, mirrors, rebrands, or sha256-gates upstream binaries here (sovereignty rule 1). This file records the tested versions + drift reference points ONLY. NEVER 'latest' silently at runtime: `celeborn doctor` compares installed-vs-pin and EXPLAINS drift, it does not block (sovereignty rule 4). Pins move only via a Celeborn release (sovereignty rule 3). The OpenCode version's SINGLE source of truth is opencode/package.json's `@opencode-ai/plugin` dependency — this file mirrors it for machine reads; bump both together.

opencode.role: the AI coding harness the board is a web client over (opencode serve /event)

ollama.install: curl -fsSL https://ollama.com/install.sh | sh   (macOS: brew install ollama)
ollama.role: the local model runtime that serves Pippin (OpenAI-compatible :11434/v1 + native :11434/api)

pippin-pm.role: the wired, highly-specified PM march-loop / board formatter — phrases board lines, NEVER decides (CELE-t283). This is the existing 'Qwen-4b PM'.

pippin-ghost.role: the 'ghost in the machine' local assistant — helpful, references celeborncode.ai/faq, analyzes dependencies, general local help. Not too smart; occasionally innocently troublesome. Improved over time via instruction sets authored by more capable models.

deprecated_tag.why: NOT a pullable registry tag — only ever resolved via a hand-made LOCAL alias (`ollama cp`). A fresh `ollama pull qwen-4b` FAILS. Retired in favor of the two real upstream tags above (sovereignty rule 1: never locally rename an upstream artifact). CELE-t374 migrates code refs (scripts/celeborn.py pm_model default, opencode/opencode.json provider key, opencode/agent/project-manager.md model line) off `qwen-4b` onto `qwen3:4b-instruct`.
